title: Tree-Distilled Deep Ensemble Member
summary: All strong current models are tree-based.
rationale: All strong current models are tree-based. Recent tabular DL work suggests parameter-efficient ensembles like TabM and tree-distilled encoders like DeepTLF can add useful diversity even when raw deep models are weaker than GBDTs.
implementation_hint: Train the current best GBDT first, export leaf-index or tree-path encodings, and feed them into a compact TabM/MLP head. Use this only as an OOF ensemble member; rank-average or logistic-stack it with XGB/CatBoost rather than replacing them.
expected_effect: May add orthogonal errors and improve blend robustness even if standalone CV is slightly below the best tree model.
risk: Standalone score may disappoint; needs careful OOF generation and regularization to avoid simply relearning the same tree signal.
